regions: South and Southeast Asia, Commonwealth of Independent States, Middle East and Africa Region (SSEA, CIS & MEA)
East Asia, Australia, and PMI Duty Free Region (EA, AU & PMI DF)

segments: South and Southeast Asia, Commonwealth of Independent States, Middle East and Africa Region (SSEA, CIS & MEA)
East Asia, Australia, and PMI Duty Free Region (EA, AU & PMI DF)

supply_chain.dependencies: Tobacco leaf sourced from Argentina, Brazil, China, India, Italy, Indonesia, Malawi, Mozambique, Philippines, Turkey, United States. Direct sourcing from farmers represents approximately 18% of PMI's global leaf requirements. Cloves are of particular importance to Indonesian business. Devices made by various electronic manufacturing services providers (EMS). Components include mechanical parts, electrical components, batteries, semiconductors, packaging materials.
supply_chain.risks: Climate change could influence weather patterns negatively impacting quality or cost of agricultural products. Supply chain disruptions due to geopolitical events (war in Ukraine, Middle East conflict). Increased demand for alternative energy sources could impact availability of deforestation-free materials. Reliance on third-party contract manufacturers and distributors may lead to disruption in distribution, quality, and availability of products. Concentration of suppliers in specific geographic areas. Illicit trade in cigarettes and smoke-free products including counterfeit, contraband, and non-compliant products.

regulatory_risks: Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) leading to increased restrictions on marketing and sale of tobacco and nicotine-containing products
WHO proposals restricting availability of smoke-free products and access to accurate information
Restrictions on or licensing of outlets permitted to sell tobacco or nicotine-containing products
Substantial and increasing tax and duty charges
Restrictions or bans on advertising, marketing and sponsorship
Display of larger health warnings, graphic health warnings and other labeling requirements
Restrictions on packaging design, including plain packaging mandates
Restrictions or bans on the display of product packaging at point of sale
Generation sales bans prohibiting sale to people born after a certain year
Requirements regarding testing, disclosure and performance standards for constituents
Disclosure, restrictions, or bans of tobacco product ingredients, including flavor bans
Increased restrictions on smoking and use of tobacco and nicotine-containing products in public and work places
Restrictions or prohibitions of novel tobacco or nicotine-containing products
Elimination of duty free sales and duty free allowances
Restrictions on importing or exporting products impacting logistics
EU Tobacco Products Directive (TPD) requirements including health warnings, characterizing flavor bans, tracking and tracing measures
EU Tobacco Excise Directive (TED) revision potentially affecting tax treatment of novel products
Plain packaging legislation adopted in Australia, France, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Canada, Denmark, Israel
Menthol and flavor bans in EU, Turkey, Brazil, Canada, and certain U.S. states and localities
Bans on display of tobacco products at retail in Australia, Canada, Russia
Product standards and restrictions on product design
FDA premarket tobacco product and modified risk tobacco product authorization requirements in U.S.
State and local laws in U.S. with stringent restrictions on sale of smoke-free products
Climate-related regulations including carbon taxation, energy price increases, EU Emissions Trading System
EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive, EU Taxonomy Regulation, EU Deforestation Regulation, EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) schemes under EU Single-Use Plastics Directive
Capital controls and foreign currency exchange constraints in jurisdictions like Argentina and Russia
Punitive tax legislation applicable to companies doing business in Russia
Trade restrictions and sanctions administered by U.S., EU, Switzerland, U.K. and other jurisdictions
OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) project and Pillar Two global minimum tax
Customs and excise tax regulations and audits in various jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Russia invasion of Ukraine leading to sanctions by U.S., EU, U.K., Switzerland, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Singapore, South Korea, Japan
Sanctions targeting Russian financial, banking, oil, military, aviation and marine sectors
Prohibition on new investment in Russian Federation by U.S. persons
Blocking sanctions on Russian political figures, oligarchs, and companies
Export bans to Russia of luxury goods, technology, and certain materials used in cigarette and heated tobacco production
Import bans from Russia including cigarettes
Sanctions on Mr. Igor Kesaev, non-majority shareholder of Megapolis Distribution B.V.
Bans on export of IQOS devices, electric accumulators, static converters to Russia
Restrictions on delivery of IT consultancy, accounting, business and management consulting services to Russia
Russian countermeasures including restrictions on export of goods, lending to foreign borrowers, repatriation of dividends, transactions with securities and real estate involving 'hostile' countries
Forced localization of foreign holding companies in Russia (JSC TK Megapolis case)
Trade restrictions and sanctions on Iran, North Korea, Syria, Belarus
U.S. OFAC and Department of State administered sanctions
State pension fund divestment legislation in U.S. targeting companies doing business in sanctioned countries
Potential retaliatory action by Russian government including nationalization of foreign businesses or assets
